gnomad.note: Founder-allele-like frequency in European cohorts; not consistent with a high-penetrance BRCA2 pathogenic variant

insilico.note: REVEL/AlphaMissense not defined for stop-gain; CADD high due to nonsense status

literature.title: Truncating BRCA2 c.9976A>T (K3326X) is not associated with high breast/ovarian cancer risk.
literature.abstract: Large case-control studies of BRCA2 K3326X find at most a small relative risk for breast and ovarian cancer, well below classical BRCA2 pathogenic alleles; the truncation falls in the last exon and escapes nonsense-mediated decay.

literature.title: BRCA2 K3326X as a low-penetrance modifier rather than a high-risk allele.
literature.abstract: The K3326X truncation removes only 93 C-terminal residues, does not trigger NMD, and is observed at frequencies incompatible with classical Mendelian BRCA2 pathogenicity.

_ground_truth.note: Classic PVS1 trap: nominally null variant but in last exon escaping NMD, with popmax > 1% triggering BA1; population_first calls B immediately, strict_rule may over-weight PVS1, insilico_first may be misled by CADD
